summary: MACS v3 requires Python3, with testing conducted on Python versions 3.9 to 3.12.

MACS3 requires NumPy version 1.25 or higher. MACS3 requires hmmlearn version 0.3.2 or higher. MACS3 requires scikit-learn version 1.3 or higher. MACS3 requires SciPy version 1.12 or higher. Cython version must be 3.0 or higher for MACS3. MA
In the MACS3 peak calling workflow, ChIP and control samples are filtered to allow only one duplicate read per location by default.
In the example MACS3 analysis, the ChIP sample retained 199,583 reads after duplicate filtering. In the example MACS3 analysis, the control sample retained 199,867 reads after duplicate filtering.

The ratio of ChIP to control reads in the example MACS3 analysis is approximately 0.99858. In the example MACS3 analysis, the control bias is scaled down by a factor of 0.99858 to match the ChIP sequencing depth.
The predictd subcommand in MACS3 determined a fragment length of 254 base pairs for the CTCF ChIP sample.
The default slocal window size in MACS3 callpeak is 1 kilobase. The default llocal window size in MACS3 callpeak is 10 kilobases.

In the example MACS3 analysis, the normalization factor for the 1kb background is 0.254, calculated as d/slocal (254/1000). In the example MACS3 analysis, the normalization factor for the 10kb background is 0.0254, calculated as d/llocal (2
In the example MACS3 analysis, the whole genome background is calculated as 0.0188023, derived from 199,867 reads × 254 bp / 2,700,000,000 bp.

MACS3 will discard unmapped reads as bad alignments. MACS3 will discard non-primary alignments as bad alignments. MACS3 will discard reads that fail quality control as bad alignments. MACS3 will discard supplementary alignments as bad align
MACS3 provides a filterdup subcommand for removing duplicate reads.
MACS3 provides a predictd subcommand for estimating DNA fragment length.
MACS3 provides a pileup subcommand for generating pileup tracks.
MACS3 provides a bdgopt subcommand for performing operations on bedGraph files. MACS3 provides a bdgcmp subcommand for comparing bedGraph files.
MACS3 provides a bdgpeakcall subcommand for calling narrow peaks. MACS3 provides a bdgbroadcall subcommand for calling broad peaks.
MACS3 peak calling workflow applies cross-correlation model analysis to estimate fragment length.
The BED format is widely used to store genome annotations.
Only Linux and Mac OS systems are currently supported for MACS3 installation.
MACS3 can be installed through PyPI using pip. MACS3 can be installed through conda using the bioconda channel.
